name: India
welcome: Namaste! Welcome to India — a land of colours, elephants, and amazing spices!
summary: India is a vast and wonderful country in southern Asia.
It is home to more than 1.4 billion people — more than any other country on Earth!
India is famous for its colourful festivals, ancient temples, spicy food, and majestic tigers.

LOCATION.intro: Let's find India on the map — it's a big one!

items.title: India in Asia
narration: India is a large triangle-shaped country pointing south into the Indian Ocean.
The tall Himalaya mountains protect India in the north.
India is surrounded by the Arabian Sea, the Bay of Bengal, and the Indian Ocean.

LANDMARKS.intro: India has breathtaking monuments and ancient wonders!

items.title: Taj Mahal
narration: The Taj Mahal is one of the most beautiful buildings in the world!
It was built by an emperor as a gift of love for his wife.
It is made of shining white marble and took 22 years to build.

items.title: Hawa Mahal
narration: The Hawa Mahal is called the Palace of Winds!
It has 953 tiny windows, so cool breezes can flow through.
It is bright pink — the colour of the beautiful city of Jaipur!

ANIMALS.intro: India is home to some of the world's most magnificent animals!

items.title: Bengal Tiger
narration: The Bengal tiger is India's national animal — and it is magnificent!
Its orange fur with black stripes helps it hide in tall grass.
India protects its tigers in special forests called Tiger Reserves.

items.title: Asian Elephant
narration: Asian elephants are loved and respected throughout India.
Elephants have been part of Indian culture for thousands of years.
They are very intelligent and can remember places and friends for decades!

items.title: Indian Peacock
narration: The peacock is India's national bird — and one of the most beautiful birds alive!
The male peacock spreads its giant feathers like a colourful fan to impress females.
Peacocks can actually fly, even with those huge tail feathers!

FOOD.intro: Indian food is bursting with amazing spices and flavours!

items.title: Samosa
narration: Samosas are crispy, triangle-shaped pastries filled with spiced potatoes and peas!
They are one of India's most popular snacks — sold everywhere on the streets.
Dip them in green mint chutney for an extra zing!

items.title: Biryani
narration: Biryani is a fragrant, layered rice dish cooked with spices and vegetables or meat.
It smells incredible from the saffron, cardamom, and cinnamon inside.
Every region in India makes its own special version of biryani!

FESTIVALS.intro: India celebrates some of the most colourful festivals in the world!

items.title: Holi — Festival of Colours
narration: Holi is the most colourful festival on Earth!
People throw bright coloured powder and water at each other — everyone gets covered!
It celebrates the arrival of spring and the victory of good over evil.

items.title: Diwali — Festival of Lights
narration: Diwali is the Festival of Lights — one of the happiest times of the year!
People light thousands of small oil lamps called diyas to brighten the night.
Fireworks fill the sky and families share sweets and gifts.

LANGUAGE.intro: India has 22 official languages! The most common is Hindi.

items.title: Namaste — Hello
narration: Namaste is a warm greeting in Hindi — it means I respect you!
You place your hands together and bow slightly as you say it.
Say it with me — na-mah-STAY!

FUN_FACTS.intro: India is full of incredible facts!

items.title: Invented Numbers
narration: India gave the world the number system we use today — including the number zero!
Without India's invention of zero, computers and maths would be impossible.
India also invented chess, yoga, and the game of snakes and ladders!

items.title: Himalaya Mountains
narration: The Himalayas form the highest mountain range on Earth, bordering northern India.
Mount Everest, the world's tallest peak, is in this range.
The word Himalaya means House of Snow in Sanskrit.
